bids.reasoning: At time fraction 0.0, it is beneficial for me to open with an offer that maximizes my utility. The opponent's initial offer proposed the minimum possible utility for me across all issues, indicating they are likely anchoring to their own maximum utility scenario. I will respond by anchoring the negotiation with my ideal outcome on all issues.

It is very early in the negotiation (t=0.02). The opponent started with the lowest possible utility for me and made a microscopic concession on issueD. I will reciprocate with a minor concession of my own to keep negotiations moving, switching issueC to its second highest value (valueB) while holding on to my absolute best options on the highly weighted issueA and the remaining issues.

The current time is very early (t=0.04). The opponent has repeated their prior offer, which provides me with very little utility. I will make a marginal concession on issueC (moving from valueB to valueD, which still yields strong utility for me) while holding firm on the other issues to evaluate their flexibility and priorities.

The negotiation is in its very early stages (t=0.06), and the opponent has started with offers that provide extremely low utility for me (only values E and F). Since my last concession on issueC was met with regression to their opening position, I will retract my concession on issueC and instead probe issueB by proposing valueB for it while keeping my other high-importance issues at maximum utility. This maintains a very high utility for me while signaling a willingness to explore different tradeoffs.

The current time is very early (t=0.08), but the opponent is continually offering highly disadvantageous packages (mostly valueF and valueE across all issues). To encourage movement without sacrificing too much utility, I will slightly adjust my offer on issueB to 'valueC' while holding firm on the other issues where my utility is maximized. This maintains my overall utility above 0.82 while testing the opponent's willingness to depart from their opening position.

The negotiation is still in its very early stages (t=0.1), and the opponent has consistently offered combinations that provide me with almost minimal utility. Having already explored slight concessions on issueB and issueC, I will now test slightly lower-utility values for issueD (valueC), while maintaining my top preferences for all other issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC) to see if this prompts any movement from the opponent.

The negotiation is still in the early stages (t=0.12), and the opponent remains stubbornly anchored to their initially proposed values (F on most issues). I will maintain a high target utility (above 0.8) and continue making minor concessions across different issues to signal flexibility while searching for areas where the opponent might be willing to compromise. I will now propose a slight concession on issueD to valueB, keeping the others at my highest utilities.

The negotiation is still in the early stages (t=0.14) and the opponent is holding very firm on mostly 'valueF' and occasionally 'valueE' across all issues, which yields extremely low utility for me. To explore potential trades without significantly sacrificing my overall payoff, I will maintain my top values on less impactful issues while offering a small concession on 'issueA', my highest-weighted issue, down to 'valueB' (0.79 utility). This keeps my overall utility high while probing for any flexibility from the opponent.

The opponent is consistently offering their initial combination (mostly valueF with minor variation to valueE on issueD), which provides me with a very low utility. The negotiation is still in the early stages (t=0.18), but the opponent has remained stubbornly fixed on an extremely low-utility offer for me (valueF for issues A/B/C and valueE for D). Since it's too early for significant concessions given their lack of movement, I will propose another combination that maintains my overall utility around 0.75, making minor concessions on issueC and issueD to search for an opening.

The opponent is remaining rigid, alternating between almost completely unfavorable offers (issueD: valueF and valueE), showing no signs of meaningful concession towards mutual benefit. As we are early in the negotiation (t=0.2), I will maintain a high utility threshold (above 85% of my maximum) while slightly modifying my offer on issueA and issueC to explore potential areas of compromise without sacrificing my most critical priorities.

The opponent has been almost entirely rigid, repeatedly offering combinations featuring valueF for issues A, B, and C, and value E/F for issue D, making minimal (if any) movement. Because it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.22) and their offers provide me with minimal utility, I will maintain a firm stance. I will explore a new combination of slight concessions on issueB and issueC to see if they are responsive, while holding firm on issueA (my most important issue) and issueD.

The opponent has remained completely uncompromising, persistently bidding combinations of valueF and valueE (which hold the lowest utility for me). I will offer a new combination by conceding slightly on issueA and issueB while holding firm on issueC and issueD, targeting a utility of roughly 0.76 to see if this elicits any movement.

The opponent is holding firm on offering extremely low-utility bids for me, primarily sticking to the lowest values across all issues. Since the time fraction is still early (t=0.26), I will persist in seeking a compromise that heavily favors my utility but explores different combinations of my second and third-best options across issues B and C. This allows me to gauge what variations might prompt the opponent to finally concede from their rigid position while maintaining my utility above 0.75.

The opponent is holding extremely firm at their original opening values (F for all issues, with occasional minor variations on issueD), showing no signs of meaningful concession. I will propose a new mix slightly conceding on issues A and B to maintain a strong expected utility while signaling a willingness to find a compromise.

The opponent continues to hold extremely firm to their lowest-utility offers, repeating the same values for nearly all issues without providing any concessions. We are approaching the mid-game (t=0.38) and the opponent has stubbornly adhered to their initial position, offering little to no meaningful concessions.
